name: azure-appconfiguration-java
description: Azure App Configuration SDK for Java. Centralized application configuration management with key-value settings, feature flags, and snapshots.
capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete key-value configuration settings
Implement feature flags with percentage-based rollouts
Manage configuration snapshots for immutable point-in-time views
Use secret references to integrate with Azure Key Vault
Apply labels for environment-based configuration separation
Leverage conditional operations with ETags for optimistic concurrency
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing code examples for Azure App Configuration SDK. No executable code was scanned. Static analysis found no suspicious patterns. Safe to publish.
valueStatement: This skill provides code examples and patterns for using the Azure App Configuration SDK in Java applications. It helps developers implement centralized configuration management with feature flags and snapshots.
userTitle: Manage Azure App Config in Java
limitations: Requires an Azure subscription and App Configuration store
Cannot create or manage the App Configuration store itself
Authentication requires Azure credentials or connection strings
Network connectivity to Azure is required

useCases.title: Manage Environment Configurations
useCases.description: DevOps engineers can organize and manage configuration settings across development, staging, and production environments using labels and key patterns.

useCases.title: Build Cloud-Native Java Applications
useCases.description: Java developers can implement dynamic configuration loading and feature management in cloud-native applications without redeploying code.

useCases.title: Implement Feature Rollouts
useCases.description: Teams can gradually rollout features using percentage-based filters and conditional feature flags for A/B testing.

promptTemplates.title: Connect to Azure App Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I connect to Azure App Configuration using Java? Show me both connection string and Entra ID authentication.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up the client with different authentication methods

promptTemplates.title: CRUD Configuration Settings
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create and retrieve a configuration setting with a label in Java using the Azure App Configuration SDK.

promptTemplates.title: Implement Feature Flags
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a feature flag with percentage rollout using the Azure App Configuration Java SDK.

promptTemplates.title: Create Configuration Snapshots
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I create a configuration snapshot for a release in Java? Show the complete workflow.

bestPractices: Use Entra ID authentication instead of connection strings for production environments
Use labels to separate configurations by environment (Dev, Staging, Production)
Use ETags with conditional operations to prevent concurrent modification conflicts
antiPatterns: Hardcoding connection strings in source code
Storing sensitive values directly without using Key Vault secret references
Storing large or binary data in configuration values

faq.question: What is Azure App Configuration?
faq.answer: Azure App Configuration is a managed service in Azure that provides centralized management of application settings and feature flags. It helps separate configuration from code and enables dynamic changes without redeployment.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure App Configuration?
faq.answer: You can authenticate using Entra ID (recommended), connection strings, or managed identities. Entra ID is preferred for production as it eliminates the need to store secrets in code or configuration files.

faq.question: What are labels in Azure App Configuration?
faq.answer: Labels are dimensional filters that allow you to organize configuration settings. Common use cases include separating settings by environment (Development, Staging, Production) or by application version.

faq.question: What are feature flags?
faq.answer: Feature flags are configuration settings that enable conditional feature activation. They support various filters including percentage-based rollouts, time-based activation, and custom targeting for gradual feature releases.

faq.question: What are configuration snapshots?
faq.answer: Configuration snapshots are immutable point-in-time views of configuration settings. They are useful for creating consistent configuration sets for releases, ensuring all settings are from the same point in time.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free. Azure App Configuration has its own pricing based on the number of operations. Refer to Azure pricing documentation for current costs.
